data_in.description: GNPS public mass spectral library (21-08-2023 no-propagated version)
YAML configuration file with pipeline settings and filter parameters

data_out.description: Cleaned mass spectral library in matchms-compatible format
Processing report documenting filter operations, metadata repairs, and statistics

domain_knowledge: Mass spectra library curation requires harmonization across multiple metadata fields (SMILES, InChI, InChIKey, parent mass, precursor m/z, adduct, ion mode) which must be internally consistent and chemically plausible.
Parent mass is properly calculated from monoisotopic mass, not molar mass; incorrect molar-mass-based parent masses are a common annotation error in public libraries.
Salts measured in mass spectrometers often fragment into different ionic forms, requiring repair of SMILES and adduct annotations by validating against parent mass and ion combinations.
SMILES-to-InChI conversion via RDKit enables validation that annotations are structurally consistent; mismatches indicate annotation errors and can guide repair or removal.
Precursor m/z, parent mass, and adduct are related by stoichiometry; inconsistencies among these fields or with ion mode indicate curation errors that must be repaired or flagged for removal.

assess whether repair functions preserved spectral integrity (peaks, intensities) while correcting metadata
evaluate whether the final spectrum count (reported vs. actual) matches expected yield from filter steps described in pipeline documentation
review processing log for any warnings or anomalies during PubChem lookups or RDKit structure validation steps
assess whether parent mass calculations are monoisotopic (not molar mass) in final output by spot-checking 10+ examples

inputs: GNPS public mass spectral library (21-08-2023 no-propagated version)
YAML configuration file with pipeline settings and filter parameters
methodology_summary: Load GNPS library and apply basic metadata harmonization filters to remove spectra with missing core fields.
Derive missing structure annotations (SMILES, InChI, InChIKey) from compound names via PubChem lookup with parent mass validation.
Repair annotation errors in SMILES (salts), parent mass (molar vs. monoisotopic), adduct, and non-matching metadata fields by checking consistency against measured mass and chemical rules.
Validate all structure annotations by cross-checking SMILES/InChI/InChIKey consistency using RDKit and requiring matching parent mass, adduct, precursor m/z, and ion mode.
Apply removal filters for spectra failing validation, normalize peak intensities, and export cleaned library with processing statistics.

YAML configuration file with pipeline settings and filter parameters

title: Run full matchms library cleaning pipeline on GNPS public mass spectral library
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load the GNPS public mass spectral library (21-08-2023 no-propagated version) using matchms in Python 3.9–3.12. 2. Apply basic metadata harmonization filters (removal of spectra lacking core metadata fields). 3. Derive missing metadata from other fields, including SMILES/InChI/InChIKey lookup from compound names via PubChem with parent mass validation. 4. Repair annotation errors: correct SMILES of salts by checking parent mass against salt ion combinations, repair parent mass when calculated from molar mass instead of monoisotopic mass, and repair adduct and parent mass based on SMILES and precursor m/z matching. 5. Repair non-matching annotations by selecting the field matching parent mass. 6. Validate all structure annotations by checking SMILES/InChI/InChIKey consistency using RDKit. 7. Apply removal filters requiring valid annotations with consistent metadata, matching parent mass to SMILES monoisotopic mass, minimum number of high-intensity peaks, and consistency among adduct, precursor m/z, parent mass, and ion mode. 8. Normalize peak intensities. 9. Export the cleaned spectral library and generate a processing report documenting all filter operations and statistics.
